message:
feat(wezterm+simulation): tick 157 MockWezterm::inject_with_cx + Scenario resize-timeline cx siblings (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Eighth post-storage tick. Closes the prerequisite flagged in
tick 156: MockWezterm::inject had no cx-first sibling, which
blocked the simulation Scenario resize-timeline helpers from
getting cx siblings. This tick lands both.

Prerequisite (wezterm.rs):
- MockWezterm::inject_with_cx — threads caller cx through the
  `panes.write_with_cx(cx)` RwLock acquire so a cancelled parent
  cx interrupts the lock wait rather than blocking.
- MockWezterm::inject_output_with_cx — convenience wrapper
  routing through `inject_with_cx` for the common
  append-output case.

Deferred-tick follow-up (simulation.rs):
- Scenario::execute_until_with_resize_timeline_with_cx —
  pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()` only. The resize-timeline body
  is 150+ LOC of stage-level probes deliberately kept
  deterministic for flamegraph reproducibility; per-event cx
  seams would drift the telemetry vs. the legacy path. Callers
  needing per-event cancellation should use the non-timeline
  variants (`execute_until_with_cx`) instead. Design intent
  documented in the doc-comment.
- Scenario::execute_all_with_resize_timeline_with_cx — thin
  composite over `execute_until_with_resize_timeline_with_cx`.

Error shape: cancellation folds into `crate::Error::Runtime` with
a "cancelled" message, matching `execute_until_with_cx`, so
callers can unify their error paths.

Verified via fork bypass: 356/356 simulation:: + wezterm:: tests
pass (185 simulation + 171 wezterm).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -941,6 +941,50 @@ impl Scenario {
         self.execute_until_with_resize_timeline(mock, self.duration)
             .await
     }
+
+    /// ft-xbnl0.2.3 Cx-first sibling of
+    /// [`Scenario::execute_until_with_resize_timeline`].
+    ///
+    /// Pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()` gates entry to the long
+    /// instrumentation loop (150+ LOC of stage-level probes). A
+    /// mid-execution cx cancel will not interrupt the current
+    /// iteration — the resize-timeline body is deliberately
+    /// deterministic for flamegraph reproducibility, so per-event
+    /// cx seams would drift the telemetry compared to the legacy
+    /// path. Callers that need per-event cancellation should use
+    /// the non-timeline variants (`execute_until_with_cx`).
+    ///
+    /// The returned Err variant mirrors the shape of
+    /// `execute_until_with_cx` cancellation errors so callers can
+    /// unify their error paths.
+    #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]
+    pub async fn execute_until_with_resize_timeline_with_cx(
+        &self,
+        cx: &crate::cx::Cx,
+        mock: &MockWezterm,
+        elapsed: Duration,
+    ) -> Result<(usize, ResizeTimeline)> {
+        cx.checkpoint().map_err(|err| {
+            crate::Error::Runtime(format!(
+                "execute_until_with_resize_timeline cancelled: {err}"
+            ))
+        })?;
+        self.execute_until_with_resize_timeline(mock, elapsed).await
+    }
+
+    /// ft-xbnl0.2.3 Cx-first sibling of
+    /// [`Scenario::execute_all_with_resize_timeline`].
+    ///
+    /// Thin composite over `execute_until_with_resize_timeline_with_cx`.
+    #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]
+    pub async fn execute_all_with_resize_timeline_with_cx(
+        &self,
+        cx: &crate::cx::Cx,
+        mock: &MockWezterm,
+    ) -> Result<(usize, ResizeTimeline)> {
+        self.execute_until_with_resize_timeline_with_cx(cx, mock, self.duration)
+            .await
+    }
 }
 
 // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
